item.text:
Implementation plan:
1. Baseline the current Loom portability behavior by running the scoped tests and inspecting the specific Loom storage/search/migration seams that differ between SQLite and PostgreSQL.
2. Implement the portability fixes inside the approved file set only: shared Loom conformance coverage first, then the minimum backend/API/migration adjustments those tests expose.
3. Re-run the Loom-targeted test commands, fix regressions within scope, and stop at a meaningful checkpoint with exact diffs, commands, and residual risk.

items.text: Baseline current Loom portability behavior and identify concrete backend drift within the scoped files
Implement shared Loom conformance coverage and minimal backend/API/migration/filepath fixes required for parity
Run scoped Loom tests and summarize checkpoint, remaining risks, and changed files

item.text: The baseline confirms the packet gap: the existing Loom tests all pass because there is almost no portability coverage yet. I’m drilling into the search/mapping helpers next so I can add conformance tests that expose real backend drift and then fix only the minimal scoped code.

items.text: Baseline current Loom portability behavior and identify concrete backend drift within the scoped files
Implement shared Loom conformance coverage and minimal backend/API/migration/filepath fixes required for parity
Run scoped Loom tests and summarize checkpoint, remaining risks, and changed files

item.aggregated_output:
async fn list_loom_edges_for_block(
        &self,
        workspace_id: &str,
        block_id: &str,
    ) -> StorageResult<Vec<LoomEdge>> {
        let rows: Vec<LoomEdgeRow> = sqlx::query_as(
            r#"
            SELECT
                edge_id,
                workspace_id,
                source_block_id,
                target_block_id,
                edge_type,
                created_by,
                created_at,
                crdt_site_id,
                source_document_id,
                source_text_block_id,
                offset_start,
                offset_end
            FROM loom_edges
            WHERE workspace_id = $1
              AND (source_block_id = $2 OR target_block_id = $2)
            ORDER BY created_at ASC
            "#,
        )
        .bind(workspace_id)
        .bind(block_id)
        .fetch_all(&self.pool)
        .await?;

        rows.into_iter()
            .map(|row| self.map_loom_edge_row(row))
            .collect()
    }

item.text: I’m moving from baseline into code now. The first patch will add the shared Loom conformance suite and the smallest backend fixes it requires: filtered sorted-view parity, tokenized PostgreSQL Loom search over the same portable fields, and filesystem/migration assertions inside the allowed scope.

I’ve added the new shared Loom conformance surface. Now I’m patching the backend seams those tests are meant to lock down: filtered sorted-view behavior in both adapters, and PostgreSQL search token matching across the same portable fields SQLite already exposes.

The portability suite and backend patches are in place. I’m running formatting and the Loom-targeted tests now; if anything breaks, I’ll keep iterating inside the same scoped files until the new coverage is green or a concrete blocker appears.

item.text: The first compile pass found a straightforward `QueryBuilder` lifetime issue in the new PostgreSQL search token loop. I’m switching those binds to owned strings and rerunning immediately.

item.text:
Implemented a meaningful Loom portability checkpoint and stopped at the remaining out-of-scope governance blocker.

Files I changed in this turn:
- [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L573)
  Added `run_loom_storage_conformance` covering Loom CRUD, dedup lookups, view/search filters, sorted-view parity, source-anchor round-trips, metric recompute checks, and delete behavior across both backends. Also added Loom migration portability assertions at [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L1998) and [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L2077).
- [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs#L2407)
  Made sorted Loom views honor the same filters as the other view types before groups are returned.
- [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L331)
  Added portable search-token normalization, then updated [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L1980) so sorted views respect filters and [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L2264) so Loom search matches tokens across `title`, `original_filename`, and `derived_json`.
- [src/backend/handshake_core/src/loom_fs.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/loom_fs.rs#L41)
  Added asset-path invariance tests for original/preview/proxy/fallback tiers.
- [src/backend/handshake_core/tests/storage_conformance.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/storage_conformance.rs#L32)
  Wired the new Loom conformance helper into both SQLite and PostgreSQL integration runs.

I did not touch the schema-registry isolation files you listed. The worktree already had unrelated dirty files outside this scope; I left them untouched.

Tests run:
- `cargo fmt -p handshake_core`
- `cargo test -p handshake_core loom` -> passed
- `cargo test -p handshake_core --test storage_conformance` -> passed
- `just gov-check` -> failed on an unrelated governance gap:
  `feature_id=FEAT-LOCUS-WORK-TRACKING`
  `gap_stub_id=WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Artifact-Family-v1`

Remaining risk:
- PostgreSQL Loom search is now semantically aligned for token/filter membership, but it still returns backend-local `score=0.0` rather than ranked parity.
- Sorted-view group `limit/offset` is still chosen before empty filtered groups are discarded, so strict filtered-group pagination could need a follow-up if that path becomes product-critical.
- `just gov-check` is blocked by the unrelated governance reference above; I did not patch governance outside the packet scope.
